summary: Ukrainian forces are deploying advanced drone systems, including the SHADOX reconnaissance-strike UAV from Black Forest Systems and the multifunctional Banderyk-24 platform from Drone Space Labs, both designed for modular, adaptable combat roles. Iranian-backed militias have launched fiber-optic FPV drone attacks on U.S. bases near Baghdad, while Ukrainian forces are using similar FPV drones to systematically destroy Russian logistics, supply lines, and rear infrastructure, causing widespread disruption. Russian forces are countering with systems like the "Yolka" anti-drone platform, which has successfully intercepted Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs such as the "Hornet." Meanwhile, humanoid Phantom MK-1 robots from Foundation are being tested in Ukraine, signaling a new phase in unmanned warfare. Ukrainian FPV tactics include coordinated strikes on artillery positions, infantry engagements, and psychological operations, while Russian forces are adapting with new antenna technologies and air defense measures.
